::: {.r-fit-text}
## **🎯 Goal: Learn how to wrangle with units**
:::


::: {.cell}

:::


# Dimensions and Units

## In PK-Sim and MoBi

PK-Sim uses the concept of Dimensions and Units

- A **dimension** represents the type of quantity (*e.g.* Volume, Mass, Concentration)
- A **unit** is one of several ways to measure a dimension (*e.g.* L, mL; kg, g; mg/L, µg/mL)

. . .

The software uses a **base unit** for each dimension

- Volume is in L
- Mass is in kg
- Concentration (molar) is in µmol/L

## Base vs Display Units

:::{.callout-note}
Although users can set or visualize a quantity in different units, `OSPS` always uses the base unit for calculations.
:::

. . .

:::{.callout-important}
The base units are often different from the units displayed by default in PK-Sim. Those are called the **display units**.
:::

. . .

[Full List of Dimensions and Units](https://docs.open-systems-pharmacology.org/appendix/appendix#a.1.-dimensions-and-base-units)

## In `{ospsuite}`

`{ospsuite}` uses the same concept of Dimensions and Units.

All Dimensions and Units available are listed in the package.



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ospsuite::ospDimensions
```

# Base and Display Units

## Base Units Internally

Like in PK-Sim and MoBi, `{ospsuite}` uses base units internally.
Users interact with the package through display units or any other supported unit.


::: {.cell layout-align="center" output-location='fragment'}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(ospsuite)

sim <- loadSimulation(system.file(
  "extdata",
  "Aciclovir.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
))

bmiParam <- getParameter("Organism|BMI", sim)

bmiParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|BMI
  • Value: 0.23 [kg/dm²]
  • Value Origin: Other-Standard definition

── Formula ──

  • isExplicit: TRUE
  • formula: Height>0 ? BW / (Height)^2 : 0
  • Value overrides formula: FALSE
```


:::
:::


## Unit Fields on a Parameter

:::{.callout-note title="Base and Display units are listed in Parameters"}

::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
bmiParam$unit
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "kg/dm²"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
bmiParam$displayUnit
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "kg/m²"
```


:::

```{.r .cell-code}
bmiParam$allUnits
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "kg/m²"  "kg/dm²"
```


:::
:::

:::

kg/dm² is the base unit for BMI. Display unit is kg/m².

# Convert Units

## `{ospsuite}` Unit Conversion Functions

The package provides a set of functions for easy conversion between units of a same dimension.

. . .

- `toUnit()` to convert from one unit to another
- `toBaseUnit()` to convert from any unit to the base unit
- `toDisplayUnit()` to convert from the base unit to the display unit

. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ospsuite::toUnit(
  quantityOrDimension = "Volume",
  values = 1,
  sourceUnit = "L",
  targetUnit = "mL"
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 1000
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ospsuite::toBaseUnit(quantityOrDimension = "Volume", values = 1000, unit = "mL")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 1
```


:::
:::


## Convert to Display Unit


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
bmiParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|BMI
  • Value: 0.23 [kg/dm²]
  • Value Origin: Other-Standard definition

── Formula ──

  • isExplicit: TRUE
  • formula: Height>0 ? BW / (Height)^2 : 0
  • Value overrides formula: FALSE
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
toDisplayUnit(quantity = bmiParam, values = 0.23)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 23
```


:::
:::


## Additional Arguments

In some situations, the `molWeight` and `molWeightUnit` arguments are required to convert between Weight and Molar Concentrations.

. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
ospsuite::toUnit(
  quantityOrDimension = "Concentration (mass)",
  values = 100,
  sourceUnit = "mg/L",
  targetUnit = "mol/L",
  molWeight = 225.21,
  molWeightUnit = "g/mol"
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 0.00044403
```


:::
:::



## Set Parameter Values

The function `setParameterValues()` can be used to set the value of a parameter from a specific unit.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
setParameterValues(bmiParam, 25)

bmiParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|BMI
  • Value: 25.00 [kg/dm²]
  • Value Origin: Other-Standard definition

── Formula ──

  • isExplicit: TRUE
  • formula: Height>0 ? BW / (Height)^2 : 0
  • Value overrides formula: TRUE
```


:::
:::


. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
setParameterValues(bmiParam, 25, units = "kg/m²")
bmiParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|BMI
  • Value: 0.25 [kg/dm²]
  • Value Origin: Other-Standard definition

── Formula ──

  • isExplicit: TRUE
  • formula: Height>0 ? BW / (Height)^2 : 0
  • Value overrides formula: TRUE
```


:::
:::


## Convert Then Set


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
setParameterValues(bmiParam, toBaseUnit(bmiParam, 25, unit = "kg/m²"))
bmiParam
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Organism|BMI
  • Value: 0.25 [kg/dm²]
  • Value Origin: Other-Standard definition

── Formula ──

  • isExplicit: TRUE
  • formula: Height>0 ? BW / (Height)^2 : 0
  • Value overrides formula: TRUE
```


:::
:::



## DataCombined

A `DataCombined` may hold simulation results and observed data in different units. Use `convertUnits()` to align them, or to change units for reporting.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
sim_res <- runSimulations(sim)[[1]]
filePath <- system.file(
  "extdata",
  "ObsDataAciclovir_1.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
)
dataSet <- loadDataSetFromPKML(filePath = filePath)

myDataCombined <- DataCombined$new()
myDataCombined$addSimulationResults(
  simulationResults = sim_res
)
myDataCombined$addDataSets(
  dataSet,
  names = "Observed (Vergin 1995.Iv)",
  groups = "Aciclovir"
)
```
:::

## `convertUnits()`

The function `convertUnits()` can be used to convert the units of the DataCombined.



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
convertUnits(myDataCombined, xUnit = "h", yUnit = "mg/L")
```

# Exercises

## Exercise Overview

Practice unit handling on the Aciclovir Dose parameter:

- **Access the Dose parameter.** Use `getParameter()`.
- **Inspect units.** Base unit and display unit.
- **Convert kg → mg.** Try `toUnit()` and `toDisplayUnit()`.
- **Change value.** Set Dose to 100 mg with `setParameterValues()`.
- **Verify.** Check if the stored unit changed.

## Access the Dose Parameter

Access the administration Dose from the simulation. What is the base unit for this parameter?


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code}
sim <- loadSimulation(system.file(
  "extdata",
  "Aciclovir.pkml",
  package = "ospsuite"
))
```
:::



::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
Dose <- getParameter(
  "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose",
  sim
)

Dose$unit
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "kg"
```


:::
:::


. . .

What is the display unit of the Dose?


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
Dose$displayUnit
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] "mg"
```


:::
:::



:::notes
The base unit is kg and the display unit is mg.
:::

## Convert kg to mg

By using two functions of your choice among `toUnit()`, `toBaseUnit()` and `toDisplayUnit()`, convert the Dose value from kg to mg.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
# We can use toUnit and toDisplayUnit
ospsuite::toUnit(
  quantityOrDimension = Dose,
  values = Dose$value,
  sourceUnit = "kg",
  targetUnit = "mg"
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 250
```


:::
:::



. . .


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
ospsuite::toDisplayUnit(quantity = Dose, values = Dose$value)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
[1] 250
```


:::
:::


. . .

Then, use `setParameterValues()` to change the value to 100 mg.


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
setParameterValues(Dose, 100, units = "mg")
```
:::


## Verify Stored Unit

Display the Dose object. Has the unit changed?


::: {.cell layout-align="center"}

```{.r .cell-code  code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"}
Dose
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
<Parameter>
  • Quantity Type: Parameter
  • Path: Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose
  • Value: 1.00e-04 [kg]

── Formula ──

  • isConstant: TRUE
```


:::
:::



:::notes
The unit is still in kg, as the base unit remains the same.
:::

<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
